title: Properties of Addition
essential_question: How can properties make adding easier?

pretest.question: Which shows the Commutative Property?

feedback.incorrect: Not quite. The answer is B. Commutative: order changes, sum same.

hints: Commutative means changing the ORDER (a + b = b + a, or a × b = b × a).

pretest.question: Which shows the Associative Property?

feedback.incorrect: Not quite. The answer is C. Associative: grouping changes.

hints: Associative means changing the GROUPING with parentheses — same answer.

pretest.question: Which shows the Identity Property?

feedback.incorrect: Not quite. The answer is A. Check your work step by step.

hints: Identity: adding 0 or multiplying by 1 keeps the number unchanged.

pretest.question: 8+0 equals 8 by the Identity Property.

feedback.incorrect: The answer is True.

hints: Identity: adding 0 or multiplying by 1 keeps the number unchanged.

pretest.question: Use the Associative Property: (15+25)+75 = 15+(25+75) = ___
hints: Group the friendly pair first: 25+75=100.
Try drawing a picture or using a place-value chart.

feedback.incorrect: Not quite. The answer is 115. Work through it place by place.

learn.title: The Commutative Property
learn.content: Order doesn't change the sum: a + b = b + a. This works with any numbers.
learn.tts: Order doesn't change the sum: a + b = b + a. This works with any numbers.

learn.title: The Associative Property
learn.content: Grouping doesn't change the sum: (a + b) + c = a + (b + c). Use parentheses to choose which numbers to add first.
learn.tts: Grouping doesn't change the sum: (a + b) + c = a + (b + c). Use parentheses to choose which numbers to add first.

learn.title: The Identity Property
learn.content: Adding zero keeps a number the same: a + 0 = a.
learn.tts: Adding zero keeps a number the same: a + 0 = a.

learn.title: Use Properties to Add Fast
learn.content: Commutative lets you rearrange numbers. Associative lets you group friendly pairs. Example: 27+45+73 = (27+73)+45 = 100+45 = 145.
learn.tts: Commutative lets you rearrange numbers. Associative lets you group friendly pairs. Example: 27+45+73 = (27+73)+45 = 100+45 = 145.

learn.title: Why Properties Matter
learn.content: Properties save time. They let you add mentally by making friendly sums first.
learn.tts: Properties save time. They let you add mentally by making friendly sums first.
